name: Vegetable Salad
steps: Wash all vegetables thoroughly under running water.
Chop cucumber, tomato, and onion into small pieces.
Grate the carrot and finely shred the cabbage.
In a large mixing bowl, combine all chopped vegetables.
Add green chili if you prefer a spicy taste.
Sprinkle salt, black pepper, and chaat masala over the vegetables.
Add 1 tablespoon fresh lemon juice.
Mix everything well until evenly coated.
Garnish with chopped coriander leaves.
Serve immediately for best freshness.

name: Roasted Cauliflower
steps: Preheat oven to 200°C (or heat a pan if not using oven).
Wash and cut cauliflower into medium-sized florets.
In a bowl, add oil, turmeric, chili powder, cumin powder, coriander powder, and salt.
Mix well to form a marinade.
Add cauliflower florets and coat them evenly with the spice mixture.
Spread the florets on a baking tray in a single layer.
Roast in the oven for 20–25 minutes, flipping halfway, until golden and slightly crispy.
If using a pan, cook on medium heat, stirring occasionally until evenly roasted.
Sprinkle garam masala and drizzle lemon juice.
Garnish with chopped coriander leaves and serve hot.

name: Dosa
ingredients: 1 cup dosa batter (fermented rice and urad dal batter)
steps: Heat a non-stick or cast iron tawa (griddle) on medium heat.
Lightly grease the tawa with a few drops of oil.
Pour a ladle of dosa batter onto the center of the tawa.
Spread the batter in a circular motion to form a thin crepe.
Drizzle a little oil or ghee around the edges.
Cook on medium heat for 2–3 minutes until the bottom turns golden and crisp.
Flip if desired and cook for another 1 minute (optional).
Fold the dosa and serve hot with chutney or sambar.

name: Carrot Salad
steps: Wash and peel the carrots thoroughly.
Grate the carrots using a fine grater.
Transfer the grated carrots to a mixing bowl.
Add salt, black pepper, and chaat masala.
Add lemon juice and mix well.
Add a small amount of oil if desired for better flavor.
Mix in crushed roasted peanuts for crunch.
Garnish with fresh coriander leaves.
Serve fresh for best taste.

name: Stuffed Indian Rolls
ingredients: 1 cup mixed vegetables (carrot, cabbage, capsicum, beans)
steps: Heat 1 teaspoon oil in a pan on medium heat.
Add sliced onions and sauté for 2–3 minutes until soft.
Add mixed vegetables and cook for 5–6 minutes until slightly tender.
Add turmeric, chili powder, garam masala, and salt. Mix well.
Cook for another 2–3 minutes and turn off heat.
Place a chapati or wrap on a flat surface.
Spread green chutney and tomato ketchup evenly.
Add the cooked vegetable stuffing in the center.
Garnish with chopped coriander leaves.
Roll tightly and serve warm.

name: Rice Salad
steps: Ensure the cooked rice is completely cooled and grains are separate.
In a large bowl, add the rice and gently fluff it.
Add chopped cucumber, tomato, onion, and capsicum.
Add roasted peanuts for crunch.
Sprinkle salt, black pepper, and chaat masala.
Add lemon juice and a small amount of oil.
Mix everything gently to combine without breaking the rice.
Garnish with fresh coriander leaves.
Serve fresh or slightly chilled.

name: Chickpea Salad
steps: Boil chickpeas until soft (or use pre-boiled chickpeas).
Drain excess water and let them cool.
In a large bowl, add the chickpeas.
Add chopped cucumber, tomato, and onion.
Add green chili if you prefer a spicy taste.
Sprinkle salt, black pepper, and chaat masala.
Add lemon juice and a small amount of oil.
Mix everything well until evenly combined.
Garnish with fresh coriander leaves.
Serve fresh.

name: Sparkling Tropical Fruit Juice
steps: Add pineapple and mango pieces to a blender.
Blend until smooth to make a fruit puree.
Pour the puree into a glass or mixing jug.
Add fresh orange juice and lemon juice.
Add honey or sugar if you prefer extra sweetness.
Mix well to combine all flavors.
Add ice cubes to a serving glass.
Pour the fruit mixture into the glass.
Top with chilled soda or sparkling water.
Gently stir and garnish with mint leaves. Serve immediately.

name: Cheese Vegetable Garlic Bread Sandwich
ingredients: 1/2 cup mixed vegetables (capsicum, onion, tomato, corn)
steps: Heat butter in a pan on low flame.
Add finely chopped garlic and sauté for 1–2 minutes until fragrant.
Add mixed vegetables and cook for 3–4 minutes until slightly soft.
Add salt, black pepper, and chili flakes. Mix well.
Turn off heat and let the mixture cool slightly.
Place bread slices on a flat surface.
Spread the vegetable mixture evenly on two slices.
Add grated cheese on top of the mixture.
Cover with remaining bread slices to make sandwiches.
Toast the sandwiches on a pan or sandwich maker until golden brown and crispy.
Cut and serve hot.

name: Cranberry Raspberry Orange Juice
steps: Add raspberries to a blender and blend into a smooth puree.
Strain the puree if you prefer a smoother texture (optional).
In a mixing jug, combine cranberry juice and fresh orange juice.
Add the raspberry puree to the mixture.
Add lemon juice and honey or sugar if needed.
Mix well until fully combined.
Add cold water or soda for a lighter or fizzy version (optional).
Fill a glass with ice cubes.
Pour the juice into the glass.
Garnish with mint leaves and serve chilled.

name: Paratha
steps: In a bowl, mix whole wheat flour and salt.
Add water gradually and knead into a soft dough.
Add 1 teaspoon oil and knead again until smooth.
Cover the dough and let it rest for 10 minutes.
Divide the dough into small balls.
Roll each ball into a flat circle using a rolling pin.
Heat a tawa (griddle) on medium heat.
Place the rolled paratha on the tawa.
Cook for 1–2 minutes, then flip.
Apply oil or ghee and cook both sides until golden brown spots appear.
Serve hot.

name: Paneer Tikka
steps: In a bowl, add curd, oil, ginger-garlic paste, turmeric, chili powder, garam masala, coriander powder, salt, and lemon juice.
Mix well to form a smooth marinade.
Add paneer cubes, capsicum, and onion pieces.
Coat everything evenly with the marinade.
Cover and marinate for at least 15–20 minutes.
Preheat oven or tawa on medium heat.
Arrange the marinated paneer and vegetables on skewers or directly on a pan.
Cook for 10–15 minutes, turning occasionally until slightly charred.
Brush lightly with oil if needed while cooking.
Serve hot with mint chutney.

name: Tangy Tomato Cucumber Salad
steps: Wash all vegetables thoroughly.
Slice cucumber and onion thinly, and chop tomatoes.
In a mixing bowl, combine cucumber, tomato, and onion.
Add green chili if you prefer a spicy taste.
Sprinkle salt, black pepper, and chaat masala.
Add lemon juice and mix well.
Garnish with chopped coriander leaves.
Serve fresh for best taste.

name: Hot Ginger Tea
steps: Heat water in a saucepan on medium flame.
Add crushed ginger and boil for 2–3 minutes.
Add tea leaves and let it simmer for another 1–2 minutes.
Add milk and sugar.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 2–3 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup.
Serve hot.

name: Green Bean Salad
steps: Wash and trim the green beans, then cut into small pieces.
Boil or steam the beans for 5–6 minutes until tender but still crisp.
Drain and let them cool slightly.
Heat oil in a pan and add mustard seeds; let them crackle.
Add chopped green chili and sauté briefly.
Add the cooked beans and toss for 2–3 minutes.
Turn off heat and transfer to a bowl.
Add salt, black pepper, and lemon juice.
Mix well and garnish with grated coconut and coriander leaves.
Serve warm or at room temperature.

name: Aloo Tikki
steps: Boil potatoes until soft, peel, and mash them in a bowl.
Add boiled green peas and mash lightly.
Add chopped green chili, grated ginger, and coriander leaves.
Add turmeric, chili powder, garam masala, and salt.
Mix everything well to form a smooth mixture.
Add cornflour or breadcrumbs to bind the mixture.
Shape the mixture into small flat patties (tikkis).
Heat oil in a pan on medium heat.
Place the tikkis and cook for 3–4 minutes on each side until golden and crispy.
Remove and serve hot with chutney or ketchup.

name: Vegetable Pulao
ingredients: 1/2 cup mixed vegetables (carrot, beans, peas, capsicum)
steps: Wash rice thoroughly and soak for 10–15 minutes.
Heat oil or ghee in a pan or pressure cooker.
Add cumin seeds, bay leaf, cinnamon, and cloves. Let them release aroma.
Add sliced onions and sauté until golden brown.
Add ginger-garlic paste and cook for 1 minute.
Add mixed vegetables and sauté for 3–4 minutes.
Add turmeric powder, garam masala, and salt. Mix well.
Add soaked rice and gently mix.
Add water and bring to a boil.
Cover and cook until rice is done (or pressure cook for 1–2 whistles).
Fluff the rice gently and garnish with coriander leaves.
Serve hot.

name: Dal Khichdi
steps: Wash rice and moong dal thoroughly and soak for 10 minutes.
Heat oil or ghee in a pressure cooker or pan.
Add cumin seeds and let them crackle.
Add chopped onion and sauté until soft (optional).
Add chopped tomato and cook until soft.
Add turmeric powder and salt, then mix well.
Add soaked rice and dal, and stir gently.
Add water and bring to a boil.
Cover and cook until soft (2–3 whistles in pressure cooker or simmer for 15–20 minutes).
Open and mix gently to achieve a soft consistency.
Garnish with coriander leaves and serve hot.

name: Cauliflower and Sweet Potato Soup
steps: Heat oil in a pot over medium heat.
Add chopped onion and sauté for 3–4 minutes until soft.
Add garlic and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add cauliflower florets and chopped sweet potato.
Add vegetable broth or water and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and simmer for 15–20 minutes until vegetables are soft.
Turn off heat and let it cool slightly.
Blend the mixture into a smooth soup using a blender.
Return to heat, add salt, black pepper, and cumin powder.
Simmer for 2–3 minutes and adjust consistency if needed.
Add lemon juice for a slight tang (optional).
Garnish with coriander leaves and serve hot.

name: Carrot Apple Salad
steps: Wash, peel, and grate the carrots.
Wash the apple and chop or grate it.
In a mixing bowl, combine the grated carrots and apple.
Add lemon juice and mix well to prevent the apple from browning.
Add honey if you prefer a slightly sweet taste.
Sprinkle salt and black pepper.
Add roasted peanuts or nuts for crunch (optional).
Mix everything gently until well combined.
Garnish with mint or coriander leaves.
Serve fresh.

name: Potato Paratha
steps: In a bowl, mix whole wheat flour and salt, then knead into a soft dough using water.
Add 1 teaspoon oil and knead again. Cover and rest for 10 minutes.
In another bowl, mix mashed potatoes, green chili, chili powder, garam masala, salt, and coriander leaves.
Divide dough into small balls.
Flatten a dough ball and place some potato filling in the center.
Seal and gently roll into a flat paratha.
Heat a tawa on medium heat and place the rolled paratha.
Cook for 1–2 minutes, flip, and apply oil or ghee.
Cook both sides until golden brown and slightly crispy.
Serve hot with curd, butter, or pickle.

name: Masala Noodles
ingredients: 1 cup mixed vegetables (carrot, capsicum, cabbage, beans)
steps: Boil water in a pan and cook noodles according to package instructions.
Drain the noodles and rinse lightly with water. Set aside.
Heat oil in a pan on medium heat.
Add chopped garlic and sauté for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add sliced onions and cook for 2–3 minutes.
Add mixed vegetables and stir-fry for 4–5 minutes.
Add soy sauce, tomato ketchup, black pepper, chili powder, and salt.
Mix well and cook for 1–2 minutes.
Add the cooked noodles and toss everything together.
Cook for another 2–3 minutes, mixing well.
Garnish with chopped spring onions and serve hot.

name: Payasam
steps: Wash the rice thoroughly and soak for 10–15 minutes.
Heat ghee in a pan and lightly fry cashews and raisins until golden. Set aside.
In a pot, add rice and water, and cook until the rice becomes soft.
Add milk and cook on low heat, stirring occasionally.
Let it simmer for 10–15 minutes until slightly thick.
Add sugar and mix well until dissolved.
Add cardamom powder for flavor.
Add the fried cashews and raisins.
Mix well and cook for another 2–3 minutes.
Serve warm or chilled.

name: Beans Curry
steps: Wash and chop the green beans into small pieces.
Heat oil in a pan on medium heat.
Add chopped onions and sauté until golden brown.
Add ginger-garlic paste and cook for 1 minute.
Add chopped tomatoes and cook until soft and mushy.
Add turmeric, chili powder, coriander powder, and salt. Mix well.
Add chopped beans and stir to coat with the masala.
Add water and mix well.
Cover and cook for 10–15 minutes until beans are tender.
Add garam masala and mix well.
Garnish with coriander leaves and serve hot.

name: Bhel Puri
steps: In a large mixing bowl, add puffed rice.
Add chopped potatoes, onion, and tomato.
Add tamarind chutney and green chutney.
Sprinkle chaat masala, chili powder, and salt.
Mix everything quickly and thoroughly.
Add sev and mix lightly.
Garnish with chopped coriander leaves.
Serve immediately to keep it crispy.

name: Steamed Brown Rice
steps: Rinse the brown rice thoroughly under running water.
Soak the rice in water for 20–30 minutes (optional but recommended).
Drain the soaking water.
In a pot, add rice and fresh water.
Add salt if desired.
Bring to a boil on medium heat.
Reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 25–30 minutes until rice is tender.
Turn off heat and let it rest covered for 5 minutes.
Fluff the rice gently with a fork.
Serve hot.

name: Cherry Blueberry Juice
steps: Wash cherries and blueberries thoroughly.
Remove seeds from cherries if not already pitted.
Add cherries, blueberries, and water or orange juice to a blender.
Blend until smooth.
Strain if you prefer a smoother juice (optional).
Add lemon juice and honey or sugar if needed.
Mix well.
Add ice cubes to a glass.
Pour the juice into the glass.
Garnish with mint leaves and serve chilled.

name: Potato Chaat
steps: Boil potatoes until soft, peel, and cut into small cubes.
Heat oil in a pan on medium heat.
Add cumin seeds and let them crackle.
Add potato cubes and fry for 5–7 minutes until slightly crispy.
Add salt, red chili powder, and chaat masala. Mix well.
Transfer to a serving bowl.
Add chopped onions on top.
Drizzle tamarind chutney and green chutney.
Add lemon juice and mix lightly.
Garnish with coriander leaves and serve warm.

name: Dum Bhindi
steps: Wash and dry the okra completely, then cut into small pieces.
Heat 1 tablespoon oil in a pan and sauté the okra on medium heat for 8–10 minutes until slightly crisp. Set aside.
In the same pan, add remaining oil and cumin seeds. Let them crackle.
Add chopped onions and sauté until golden brown.
Add ginger-garlic paste and cook for 1 minute.
Add chopped tomatoes and cook until soft and oil separates.
Add turmeric, chili powder, coriander powder, and salt. Mix well.
Add the sautéed okra and mix gently.
Cover and cook on low heat (dum) for 8–10 minutes.
Add garam masala and mix well.
Garnish with coriander leaves and serve hot.

name: Rava Idli
steps: Dry roast semolina in a pan on low heat for 4–5 minutes until aromatic. Let it cool.
In a bowl, mix roasted semolina, curd, and water to form a thick batter.
Add salt and mix well. Let the batter rest for 10 minutes.
Heat oil in a pan and add mustard seeds and cumin seeds. Let them crackle.
Add cashews, grated ginger, and green chili. Sauté for 1–2 minutes.
Add this tempering to the batter and mix well.
Add baking soda or Eno just before steaming and mix gently.
Grease idli molds and pour the batter into them.
Steam for 10–12 minutes until cooked.
Remove and let them cool slightly before taking out.
Serve hot with chutney or sambar.

name: Corn Pulao
steps: Wash rice thoroughly and soak for 10–15 minutes.
Heat oil or ghee in a pan or pressure cooker.
Add cumin seeds, bay leaf, cinnamon, and cloves. Let them release aroma.
Add sliced onions and sauté until golden brown.
Add ginger-garlic paste and cook for 1 minute.
Add boiled sweet corn and sauté for 2–3 minutes.
Add turmeric powder, garam masala, and salt. Mix well.
Add soaked rice and mix gently.
Add water and bring to a boil.
Cover and cook until rice is done (or pressure cook for 1–2 whistles).
Fluff the rice gently and garnish with coriander leaves.
Serve hot.

name: Pineapple Smoothie
steps: Add chopped pineapple to a blender.
Add curd (or milk) for a creamy texture.
Add ice cubes for a chilled drink.
Add honey or sugar if you prefer extra sweetness.
Add lemon juice for a slight tang (optional).
Blend everything until smooth and creamy.
Pour into a glass.
Serve immediately while chilled.

name: Lassi
steps: Add curd and cold water to a blender.
Add sugar and cardamom powder.
Add a few ice cubes.
Blend until smooth and frothy.
Pour into a glass.
Serve chilled.

name: Vegetable Tehri
ingredients: 1 cup mixed vegetables (potato, carrot, peas, beans)
steps: Wash rice thoroughly and soak for 10–15 minutes.
Heat oil or mustard oil in a pan or pressure cooker.
Add cumin seeds, bay leaf, cinnamon, and cloves. Let them release aroma.
Add sliced onions and sauté until golden brown.
Add ginger-garlic paste and cook for 1 minute.
Add mixed vegetables and sauté for 3–4 minutes.
Add turmeric, chili powder, garam masala, and salt. Mix well.
Add soaked rice and mix gently.
Add water and bring to a boil.
Cover and cook until rice is done (or pressure cook for 1–2 whistles).
Fluff gently and garnish with coriander leaves.
Serve hot.

name: Carrot Smoothie
steps: Wash, peel, and chop the carrots into small pieces.
Boil or steam the carrots for 5–7 minutes until soft (for smoother blending).
Let the carrots cool slightly.
Add carrots, curd (or milk), and water to a blender.
Add honey and cinnamon if desired.
Add a few ice cubes.
Blend until smooth and creamy.
Pour into a glass.
Serve chilled.

name: Kachumber Salad
steps: Wash all vegetables thoroughly.
Finely chop cucumber, tomatoes, and onion.
Transfer everything to a mixing bowl.
Add green chili if you prefer a spicy taste.
Add salt, black pepper, and chaat masala.
Add lemon juice and mix well.
Garnish with chopped coriander leaves.
Serve fresh.

name: Baked Mushrooms
steps: Preheat oven to 200°C.
Clean mushrooms and remove excess moisture.
In a bowl, mix butter or oil, garlic, black pepper, chili flakes, and salt.
Add mushrooms and coat them evenly with the mixture.
Place mushrooms on a baking tray in a single layer.
Sprinkle grated cheese on top (optional).
Bake for 15–20 minutes until tender and slightly golden.
Remove from oven and garnish with chopped parsley or coriander.
Serve hot.

name: Vegan Pumpkin Curry
steps: Heat oil or coconut oil in a pan on medium heat.
Add chopped onions and sauté until golden brown.
Add ginger-garlic paste and cook for 1 minute.
Add chopped tomatoes and cook until soft and mushy.
Add turmeric, chili powder, coriander powder, and salt. Mix well.
Add pumpkin cubes and mix to coat with the masala.
Add water, cover, and cook for 10–12 minutes until pumpkin is soft.
Add coconut milk and mix well.
Simmer for 5 minutes on low heat.
Add garam masala and mix well.
Garnish with coriander leaves and serve hot.

name: Malted Walnut Seed Loaf
ingredients: 2 tbsp malted powder or malt extract
steps: In a bowl, mix warm water, sugar, and yeast. Let it sit for 5–10 minutes until frothy.
In another bowl, combine whole wheat flour, all-purpose flour, malt powder, and salt.
Add the yeast mixture and oil to the flour mixture.
Knead into a soft dough.
Add chopped walnuts and seeds, and knead again to incorporate.
Cover the dough and let it rise in a warm place for 45–60 minutes until doubled in size.
Preheat oven to 180°C.
Shape the dough into a loaf and place in a greased baking tin.
Let it rest for another 10–15 minutes.
Bake for 30–35 minutes until golden brown and cooked through.
Remove and let it cool before slicing.
Serve fresh or toasted.

name: Khandvi
steps: In a bowl, mix gram flour, curd, water, turmeric, and salt to form a smooth batter without lumps.
Cook the mixture in a pan on low heat, stirring continuously.
Cook until it thickens to a smooth, spreadable consistency.
Quickly spread a thin layer of the mixture on a flat surface (plate or countertop).
Let it cool for a few minutes.
Cut into long strips and gently roll them.
Heat oil in a small pan and add mustard seeds.
Add sesame seeds and green chili, and sauté briefly.
Pour this tempering over the rolled khandvi.
Garnish with coriander leaves and grated coconut.
Serve at room temperature.

name: Rasgulla
steps: Boil milk in a pan on medium heat.
Add lemon juice gradually while stirring until the milk curdles.
Turn off heat and strain using a cloth to separate chenna (curds).
Wash the chenna under water to remove sourness.
Squeeze out excess water and let it rest for 10–15 minutes.
Knead the chenna until smooth and soft.
Divide into small balls and roll without cracks.
In a separate pan, boil water and sugar to make syrup.
Add the chenna balls to the boiling syrup.
Cover and cook for 15–20 minutes until they double in size.
Add cardamom powder for flavor (optional).
Cool and serve chilled or at room temperature.

name: Popcorn
steps: Heat oil in a deep pan on medium heat.
Add a few kernels and cover the pan.
When they pop, add the remaining kernels.
Cover the pan and shake occasionally.
Cook until popping slows down.
Turn off heat and let it rest for a minute.
Open the lid and add salt and black pepper.
Mix well and serve warm.

name: Cottage Cheese Fritters in Curry
steps: In a bowl, mix crumbled paneer, cornflour, salt, and black pepper.
Shape into small balls (koftas).
Heat oil and deep-fry the balls until golden brown. Set aside.
Heat 1 tbsp oil in a pan.
Add chopped onions and sauté until golden.
Add ginger-garlic paste and cook for 1 minute.
Add tomato puree and cook until oil separates.
Add turmeric, chili powder, coriander powder, and salt.
Add water and bring to a simmer.
Add cream or milk and mix well.
Add the fried paneer balls just before serving.
Sprinkle garam masala and garnish with coriander leaves.
Serve hot.

name: Soya Stir-Fry with Bean Sprouts
steps: Soak soy chunks in hot water for 5–10 minutes, then squeeze out excess water.
Heat oil in a pan on medium-high heat.
Add chopped garlic and sauté for 1 minute.
Add sliced onions and cook for 2–3 minutes.
Add capsicum and stir-fry for 2–3 minutes.
Add soy chunks and mix well.
Add soy sauce, black pepper, chili flakes, and salt.
Stir-fry for 3–4 minutes.
Add bean sprouts and cook for 2 minutes (keep them slightly crunchy).
Garnish with spring onions and serve hot.

name: Gujarati Sev
steps: In a bowl, mix gram flour, turmeric, chili powder, and salt.
Add 1 tablespoon oil and mix well.
Add water gradually to form a soft dough.
Heat oil in a deep pan on medium heat.
Fill the dough into a sev maker or press.
Press the dough directly into hot oil in circular motions.
Fry until crisp and golden.
Remove and drain excess oil.
Let it cool completely.
Store in an airtight container or use immediately.

name: Gujarati Khichdi
steps: Wash rice and moong dal thoroughly.
Soak for 10–15 minutes (optional).
Heat ghee or oil in a pressure cooker or pan.
Add cumin seeds and let them crackle.
Add a pinch of hing and turmeric powder.
Add rice and dal, and mix well.
Add water and salt.
Bring to a boil.
Cover and cook (2–3 whistles in pressure cooker or simmer until soft).
Mix gently to get a soft, slightly mushy consistency.
Garnish with coriander leaves if desired.
Serve hot with curd or pickle.

name: Apple Sauce
steps: Peel, core, and chop the apples into small pieces.
Add apples and water to a pan.
Cook on medium heat for 10–15 minutes until apples are soft.
Mash the apples using a spoon or blend for a smoother texture.
Add sugar or honey if desired.
Add cinnamon powder and lemon juice.
Mix well and cook for another 2–3 minutes.
Let it cool slightly.
Serve warm or chilled.

name: Handvo
ingredients: 1 cup handvo flour (mixed lentil and rice flour)
steps: In a bowl, mix handvo flour, curd, and water to form a thick batter.
Add grated bottle gourd, carrot, green chili, and ginger.
Add turmeric, salt, and sugar. Mix well.
Let the batter rest for 20–30 minutes.
Heat oil in a pan and add mustard seeds and sesame seeds.
Pour the batter into a greased baking dish or pan.
Add the tempering on top.
Bake in a preheated oven at 180°C for 30–35 minutes until golden brown.
Alternatively, cook on low flame in a covered pan until cooked through.
Let it cool slightly, cut into pieces, and serve.

name: Indian Carrot Crepe
ingredients: 1/2 cup wheat flour or besan (gram flour)
steps: In a bowl, mix flour, grated carrot, green chili, coriander leaves, cumin seeds, and salt.
Add water gradually to form a smooth, pourable batter.
Heat a non-stick pan on medium heat.
Lightly grease the pan with a few drops of oil.
Pour a ladle of batter and spread it into a thin circle.
Drizzle a little oil around the edges.
Cook for 2–3 minutes until the bottom is golden.
Flip and cook the other side for 1–2 minutes.
Remove and serve hot with chutney.

name: Cheesy Garlic Bread
steps: Preheat oven to 180°C.
In a bowl, mix butter and chopped garlic.
Spread the garlic butter evenly on bread slices.
Sprinkle grated cheese on top.
Add chili flakes, oregano, and a pinch of salt.
Place the bread on a baking tray.
Bake for 8–10 minutes until cheese melts and edges are slightly crisp.
Remove from oven and garnish with chopped coriander or parsley.
Cut into pieces and serve hot.

name: Coffee Bar Style Tea
steps: Heat water in a saucepan on medium heat.
Add tea leaves and ginger (if using).
Boil for 2–3 minutes to extract strong flavor.
Add milk and sugar.
Bring to a boil and simmer for 2–3 minutes.
Add cardamom powder for aroma (optional).
Strain into a cup.
Serve hot.

name: Curry Sauce
steps: Heat oil in a pan on medium heat.
Add chopped onions and sauté until golden brown.
Add ginger-garlic paste and cook for 1 minute.
Add tomato puree and cook until oil separates.
Add turmeric, chili powder, coriander powder, and salt.
Cook for 2–3 minutes until spices are well blended.
Add water and mix well.
Simmer for 8–10 minutes until the gravy thickens.
Add cream or milk for a richer texture (optional).
Add garam masala and mix well.
Garnish with coriander leaves.
Use as a base for vegetables, paneer, or other curries.

name: Puri
steps: In a bowl, mix whole wheat flour and salt.
Add water gradually and knead into a stiff dough.
Add 1 teaspoon oil and knead again.
Cover and let the dough rest for 10 minutes.
Divide into small balls.
Roll each ball into small circles (not too thin).
Heat oil in a deep pan on medium heat.
Slide the rolled dough into hot oil.
Gently press with a spoon so it puffs up.
Flip and fry until golden brown.
Remove and drain excess oil.
Serve hot.

name: Rasam
steps: Boil chopped tomatoes in water until soft.
Mash them lightly.
Add tamarind pulp, turmeric, rasam powder, and salt.
Add cooked dal (optional) and mix well.
Bring to a gentle boil.
In another pan, heat oil or ghee.
Add mustard seeds and let them crackle.
Add cumin seeds, garlic, hing, and curry leaves.
Pour this tempering into the rasam.
Simmer for 2–3 minutes.
Garnish with coriander leaves.
Serve hot as soup or with rice.

name: Naan
ingredients: 1/2 tsp baking soda or 1 tsp yeast
steps: In a bowl, mix flour, sugar, salt, and baking soda (or yeast).
Add curd, oil, and warm water to form a soft dough.
Knead well until smooth.
Cover and let the dough rest for 1–2 hours.
Divide into small balls.
Roll each into an oval or round shape.
Heat a tawa or pan on high heat.
Place naan on the hot surface and cook until bubbles form.
Flip and cook the other side (or cook directly over flame for char).
Brush with butter.
Garnish with coriander or garlic if desired.
Serve hot.

name: Whole Wheat Bread
steps: In a bowl, mix warm water, sugar, and yeast. Let it sit for 5–10 minutes until frothy.
In another bowl, combine whole wheat flour and salt.
Add the yeast mixture and oil to the flour.
Knead into a soft dough.
Cover and let it rise for 1–1.5 hours until doubled in size.
Punch down the dough and shape into a loaf.
Place in a greased loaf pan.
Let it rise again for 20–30 minutes.
Preheat oven to 180°C.
Bake for 30–35 minutes until golden brown.
Remove and cool completely before slicing.

name: Upma
steps: Dry roast semolina in a pan on low heat for 4–5 minutes until aromatic. Set aside.
Heat oil in a pan on medium heat.
Add mustard seeds and let them crackle.
Add cumin seeds, hing, green chili, and ginger.
Add chopped onions and sauté until soft.
Add mixed vegetables and cook for 3–4 minutes.
Add water and salt, and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and slowly add roasted semolina while stirring continuously.
Cook and stir until the mixture thickens and absorbs water.
Add lemon juice and mix well.
Garnish with coriander leaves.
Serve hot.

name: Chapati
steps: In a bowl, mix whole wheat flour and salt.
Add water gradually and knead into a soft dough.
Knead well for 5–7 minutes until smooth.
Cover and let the dough rest for 10–15 minutes.
Divide into small balls.
Roll each ball into a thin circle.
Heat a tawa on medium-high heat.
Place the rolled chapati on the hot tawa.
Cook until bubbles form, then flip.
Press lightly and cook until both sides have brown spots.
Optionally, cook directly over flame to puff.
Serve hot.

name: Olan
steps: Cook cowpeas until soft if not already cooked.
In a pan, add ash gourd and a little water.
Cook until the ash gourd becomes soft.
Add cooked cowpeas and green chilies.
Add salt and mix gently.
Pour in coconut milk and simmer on low heat.
Do not boil after adding coconut milk.
Drizzle coconut oil on top.
Add curry leaves for aroma.
Mix gently and serve warm.

name: Spicy Leaves Kuzhambu
ingredients: 2 cups leafy greens (spinach or amaranth, chopped)
1 tsp sambar powder or kuzhambu powder
steps: Wash and chop the leafy greens.
Heat oil in a pan on medium heat.
Add mustard seeds and let them crackle.
Add cumin seeds, garlic, and curry leaves.
Add chopped onions and sauté until soft.
Add turmeric, chili powder, and sambar powder.
Add tamarind pulp and water, and bring to a boil.
Add chopped leafy greens and cook until soft.
Simmer for 8–10 minutes until the gravy thickens.
Adjust salt and consistency.
Serve hot with rice.

name: Raw Sauce
steps: Add chopped tomatoes, onion, garlic, and green chili to a blender.
Blend lightly to get a slightly coarse texture.
Add lemon juice and salt.
Add a little oil if you want a smoother taste (optional).
Mix well.
Garnish with fresh coriander leaves.
Serve fresh without cooking.

name: Mixed Vegetable Curry
ingredients: 1 cup mixed vegetables (carrot, beans, peas, cauliflower, potato)
steps: Wash and chop all vegetables into small pieces.
Heat oil in a pan on medium heat.
Add chopped onions and sauté until golden.
Add ginger-garlic paste and cook for 1 minute.
Add tomato puree and cook until oil separates.
Add turmeric, chili powder, coriander powder, and salt.
Add mixed vegetables and mix well.
Add water and bring to a boil.
Cover and cook for 10–15 minutes until vegetables are soft.
Add garam masala and mix well.
Garnish with coriander leaves.
Serve hot.

name: Chakli
steps: In a bowl, mix rice flour, gram flour, sesame seeds, cumin seeds, turmeric, chili powder, and salt.
Add butter or oil and mix well.
Add water gradually to form a soft dough.
Fill the dough into a chakli maker (press).
Shape spirals on a plate or parchment paper.
Heat oil in a deep pan on medium heat.
Carefully slide the shaped chakli into hot oil.
Fry until golden brown and crisp.
Remove and drain excess oil.
Let it cool completely before storing.
Serve as a snack.

name: Tomato Soup
steps: Heat oil or butter in a pan on medium heat.
Add chopped onion and garlic, and sauté until soft.
Add chopped tomatoes and cook for 5–7 minutes until soft.
Add water or vegetable broth and bring to a boil.
Simmer for 10 minutes.
Let it cool slightly, then blend into a smooth mixture.
Strain if you want a smoother texture (optional).
Return to heat and add salt, black pepper, and sugar.
Simmer for 2–3 minutes.
Add cream if desired.
Garnish and serve hot.

name: Kappa
steps: Peel and cut tapioca into medium-sized cubes.
Wash thoroughly to remove excess starch.
Boil in water with a pinch of salt and turmeric until soft.
Drain the water.
Heat coconut oil in a pan.
Add mustard seeds and let them crackle.
Add dried red chilies, curry leaves, and green chili.
Add boiled tapioca and mix well.
Cook for 2–3 minutes to combine flavors.
Serve hot.

name: Vegetable Kurma
ingredients: 1 cup mixed vegetables (carrot, beans, peas, potato)
1/2 cup coconut milk or cashew paste
steps: Wash and chop all vegetables.
Heat oil in a pan on medium heat.
Add chopped onions and sauté until soft.
Add ginger-garlic paste and cook for 1 minute.
Add chopped tomatoes and cook until soft.
Add turmeric, chili powder, coriander powder, and salt.
Add vegetables and mix well.
Add water and cook until vegetables are tender.
Add coconut milk or cashew paste and mix well.
Simmer for 5 minutes.
Add garam masala and mix.
Garnish with coriander leaves and serve hot.

name: Beetroot Soup
steps: Heat oil or butter in a pan on medium heat.
Add chopped onion and garlic, and sauté until soft.
Add chopped beetroot and cook for 3–4 minutes.
Add water or vegetable broth and bring to a boil.
Simmer for 15–20 minutes until beetroot is soft.
Let it cool slightly, then blend into a smooth soup.
Return to heat and add salt and black pepper.
Add lemon juice for a slight tang.
Simmer for 2–3 minutes.
Add cream if desired.
Garnish and serve hot.

name: Khaman Dhokla
steps: In a bowl, mix gram flour, water, lemon juice, sugar, turmeric, salt, and oil to form a smooth batter.
Let the batter rest for 10 minutes.
Add baking soda or Eno just before steaming and mix gently.
Grease a steaming tray or plate.
Pour the batter into the tray.
Steam for 12–15 minutes until cooked.
Let it cool slightly and cut into squares.
Heat oil in a pan and add mustard seeds.
Add sesame seeds, green chilies, and curry leaves.
Pour the tempering over the dhokla.
Garnish with coriander leaves.
Serve soft and warm.

name: Gulab Jamun
steps: In a bowl, mix milk powder, flour, and baking soda.
Add ghee and mix well.
Add milk gradually to form a soft dough.
Divide into small balls and roll smoothly without cracks.
Heat oil or ghee on low-medium heat.
Fry the balls gently until golden brown.
Remove and drain excess oil.
In another pan, prepare sugar syrup by boiling sugar and water.
Add cardamom powder and rose water.
Add fried balls to warm syrup.
Let them soak for at least 15–20 minutes.
Serve warm or at room temperature.

name: Tomato Chutney
steps: Heat oil in a pan on medium heat.
Add dried red chilies and garlic, sauté briefly.
Add chopped onions and cook until soft.
Add chopped tomatoes and cook until mushy.
Let the mixture cool slightly.
Blend into a smooth or slightly coarse chutney.
In the same pan, heat a little oil.
Add mustard seeds, cumin seeds, hing, and curry leaves.
Pour this tempering over the chutney.
Mix well and serve.

name: Kashmiri Haak
steps: Wash the leafy greens thoroughly and chop roughly.
Heat mustard oil in a pan until slightly smoky, then reduce heat.
Add dried red chilies and cumin seeds.
Add chopped greens and mix well.
Add salt and water.
Cover and cook on medium heat for 10–12 minutes.
Stir occasionally until greens are soft.
Cook uncovered for a few minutes if excess water remains.
Serve hot.

name: Indian Tea
steps: Heat water in a saucepan on medium heat.
Add tea leaves and boil for 2–3 minutes.
Add milk and sugar.
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 2–3 minutes.
Strain into a cup.
Serve hot.

name: Beans and Turnips Curry
steps: Wash and chop beans and turnips into small pieces.
Heat oil in a pan on medium heat.
Add chopped onions and sauté until golden.
Add ginger-garlic paste and cook for 1 minute.
Add tomatoes and cook until soft and oil separates.
Add turmeric, chili powder, coriander powder, and salt.
Add beans and turnips, and mix well.
Add water and bring to a boil.
Cover and cook for 12–15 minutes until vegetables are tender.
Add garam masala and mix well.
Garnish with coriander leaves.
Serve hot.

name: Kulfi
steps: Heat milk in a pan on medium heat.
Simmer and stir continuously until it reduces to about half.
Add sugar and mix until dissolved.
Add milk powder and stir well to avoid lumps.
Add chopped nuts, cardamom powder, and saffron.
Cook for 2–3 more minutes.
Let the mixture cool completely.
Pour into molds or small cups.
Freeze for 6–8 hours until set.
Remove from molds and serve frozen.

name: Vada
steps: Drain soaked urad dal and grind into a thick, smooth batter using minimal water.
Transfer batter to a bowl and whisk for a few minutes to make it light and fluffy.
Add chopped green chili, ginger, curry leaves, and salt.
Heat oil in a deep pan on medium heat.
Wet your hands, take a small portion of batter, shape into a donut (with a hole).
Carefully drop into hot oil.
Fry until golden brown and crisp on both sides.
Remove and drain excess oil.
Serve hot with chutney or sambar.

name: Kachori
ingredients: 1/2 cup moong dal (soaked and coarsely ground)
steps: In a bowl, mix flour, salt, and oil. Add water gradually and knead into a stiff dough.
Cover and let the dough rest for 15 minutes.
Heat a little oil in a pan and add cumin and fennel seeds.
Add grated ginger and sauté briefly.
Add ground moong dal and cook for 5–6 minutes.
Add turmeric, chili powder, coriander powder, and salt.
Cook until mixture is dry. Let it cool.
Divide dough into small balls.
Flatten each ball, add stuffing, and seal it.
Gently roll into small discs.
Heat oil on medium-low heat.
Fry kachoris slowly until golden and crisp.
Remove and serve hot.

name: Fried Pickles
ingredients: 1 cup pickle slices (cucumber or any firm pickle)
steps: Drain excess liquid from pickle slices and pat them dry.
In a bowl, mix flour, cornflour, black pepper, chili powder, and salt.
Add water gradually to form a thick batter.
Heat oil in a deep pan on medium heat.
Dip pickle slices into the batter.
Carefully drop into hot oil.
Fry until golden and crispy.
Remove and drain excess oil.
Serve hot.

name: Dry Vegetable Curry
ingredients: 1 cup mixed vegetables (potato, carrot, beans, cauliflower)
steps: Wash and chop all vegetables into small pieces.
Heat oil in a pan on medium heat.
Add cumin seeds and let them crackle.
Add chopped onions and sauté until soft.
Add turmeric, chili powder, coriander powder, and salt.
Add mixed vegetables and mix well.
Cover and cook on low heat, stirring occasionally.
Cook for 10–15 minutes until vegetables are tender.
Add garam masala and mix.
Garnish with coriander leaves.
Serve hot.

name: Kheer
steps: Wash and soak rice for 15–20 minutes.
Heat milk in a pan on medium heat.
Add soaked rice and cook on low heat.
Stir frequently to prevent sticking.
Cook until rice becomes soft and milk thickens.
Add sugar and mix well.
Add chopped nuts, cardamom powder, and saffron.
Cook for another 5–10 minutes.
Turn off heat and let it cool slightly.
Serve warm or chilled.

name: Samosa
steps: In a bowl, mix flour, salt, and oil. Add water and knead into a stiff dough.
Cover and let the dough rest for 15 minutes.
Heat a little oil in a pan and add cumin seeds.
Add ginger and sauté briefly.
Add peas and cook for 2–3 minutes.
Add mashed potatoes and mix well.
Add turmeric, chili powder, coriander powder, and salt.
Cook for 3–4 minutes and let the filling cool.
Divide dough into balls and roll into ovals.
Cut each oval in half to form cones.
Fill with stuffing and seal edges.
Heat oil on medium heat.
Fry samosas until golden and crisp.
Remove and serve hot.

name: Mixed Fruit Bowl
steps: Wash and chop all fruits into bite-sized pieces.
Add all fruits to a bowl.
Add lemon juice to prevent browning.
Add honey if you want extra sweetness.
Sprinkle a pinch of black salt for flavor (optional).
Mix gently.
Serve fresh.

name: Pav Bhaji
steps: Heat butter and oil in a pan on medium heat.
Add chopped onions and sauté until soft.
Add ginger-garlic paste and cook for 1 minute.
Add chopped tomatoes and cook until mushy.
Add capsicum and cook for 2–3 minutes.
Add turmeric, pav bhaji masala, and salt.
Add boiled potatoes, cauliflower, and peas.
Mash everything together in the pan.
Add water as needed to adjust consistency.
Cook for 5–7 minutes, stirring continuously.
Garnish with coriander leaves.
Toast pav buns with butter on a pan.
Serve hot with lemon wedges and chopped onions.

name: Bhatura
steps: In a bowl, mix flour, baking soda, sugar, and salt.
Add curd and oil, then mix well.
Add water gradually and knead into a soft dough.
Cover and let the dough rest for 1–2 hours.
Divide into medium-sized balls.
Roll each into an oval shape.
Heat oil in a deep pan on medium heat.
Fry the bhatura until it puffs up and turns golden.
Flip and fry the other side.
Remove and drain excess oil.
Serve hot.

name: Sambar
ingredients: 1 cup mixed vegetables (carrot, drumstick, pumpkin, brinjal)
steps: Cook toor dal with turmeric until soft, then mash well.
In a pan, cook chopped vegetables in water until tender.
Add tamarind pulp, salt, and sambar powder.
Bring to a boil and simmer for 5–7 minutes.
Add mashed dal and mix well.
Adjust water for desired consistency.
Simmer for another 5 minutes.
Heat oil or ghee in a small pan.
Add mustard seeds and let them crackle.
Add cumin seeds, red chilies, curry leaves, and onions (optional).
Pour this tempering into the sambar.
Garnish with coriander leaves.
Serve hot with rice, idli, or dosa.

name: White Dhokla
steps: Soak rice and urad dal separately for 4–5 hours.
Grind into a smooth batter.
Mix both batters and add curd and salt.
Let it ferment overnight or for 8 hours.
Grease a steaming tray.
Pour batter into the tray.
Steam for 15–20 minutes until cooked.
Let it cool slightly and cut into pieces.
Heat oil and add mustard seeds.
Add sesame seeds and curry leaves.
Pour tempering over dhokla.
Garnish with coriander leaves and serve.

name: Baked Sweet Potato Fries
steps: Preheat oven to 200°C.
Wash, peel, and cut sweet potatoes into thin strips.
Place them in a bowl and add oil, salt, and spices.
Mix well to coat evenly.
Spread fries in a single layer on a baking tray.
Bake for 20–25 minutes, flipping halfway through.
Cook until edges are crisp and golden.
Serve hot.

name: Bonda
steps: In a bowl, mix mashed potatoes, green chili, ginger, coriander, turmeric, and salt.
Shape the mixture into small balls.
In another bowl, mix gram flour, chili powder, salt, and water to form a thick batter.
Heat oil in a deep pan on medium heat.
Dip each potato ball into the batter.
Carefully drop into hot oil.
Fry until golden and crisp.
Remove and drain excess oil.
Serve hot with chutney.

name: Dabeli
steps: Heat oil in a pan and add mashed potatoes.
Add dabeli masala and salt, and mix well.
Cook for 3–4 minutes and set aside.
Slice pav buns horizontally.
Spread tamarind chutney on one side and garlic chutney on the other.
Add the prepared potato mixture inside.
Top with onions, peanuts, pomegranate seeds, and sev.
Close the pav and press gently.
Toast with butter on a pan until crisp.
Serve hot.

name: Black Bean Salad
ingredients: 1 cup black beans (cooked or canned, drained)
steps: Rinse and drain black beans if using canned.
Add beans, corn, tomato, cucumber, and onion to a bowl.
Add lemon juice and olive oil.
Add salt and black pepper.
Mix everything well.
Garnish with chopped coriander or parsley.
Serve fresh.

name: Strawberry Salad
steps: Wash and slice strawberries.
Wash and chop lettuce or spinach.
Add strawberries, greens, cucumber, and nuts to a bowl.
In a small bowl, mix olive oil, lemon juice, honey, salt, and pepper.
Pour dressing over the salad.
Toss gently to combine.
Serve fresh.

name: Blackberry Jam
steps: Wash blackberries thoroughly.
Add blackberries to a pan and mash lightly.
Add sugar and lemon juice.
Cook on medium heat, stirring continuously.
Simmer for 15–20 minutes until thickened.
Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.
Check consistency (it thickens more on cooling).
Let it cool completely.
Store in a clean, airtight jar.
Serve with bread or desserts.

name: Chai Raisin Oatmeal Cookies
steps: Preheat oven to 180°C.
In a bowl, mix oats, flour, baking soda, salt, and spices.
In another bowl, mix butter (or oil) and sugar until smooth.
Add milk and mix well.
Combine wet and dry ingredients.
Add raisins and mix gently.
Scoop small portions onto a baking tray.
Flatten slightly.
Bake for 12–15 minutes until golden.
Let them cool before serving.

name: Strawberry Tart
steps: Preheat oven to 180°C.
In a bowl, mix flour and sugar.
Add cold butter and rub until crumbly.
Add cold water and form a dough.
Roll the dough and place in a tart pan.
Prick with a fork and bake for 15–20 minutes until golden.
Let the crust cool completely.
Spread cream or custard evenly on the base.
Arrange sliced strawberries on top.
Sprinkle sugar lightly if desired.
Chill for 15–20 minutes before serving.
Serve fresh.

name: Fresh Tomato Sauce
steps: Heat olive oil in a pan on medium heat.
Add chopped garlic and sauté for 1 minute.
Add chopped tomatoes and cook until soft.
Mash lightly or blend for smoother texture.
Add salt, black pepper, and herbs.
Add sugar if tomatoes are too sour.
Simmer for 5–7 minutes until slightly thick.
Garnish with fresh basil if available.
Serve warm.

name: Tofu Vindaloo
steps: Heat oil in a pan on medium heat.
Add chopped onions and sauté until golden.
Add ginger-garlic paste and cook for 1 minute.
Add chopped tomatoes and cook until soft.
Add chili powder, cumin, coriander, turmeric, and salt.
Add vinegar and mix well.
Add tofu cubes and coat with the masala.
Add water and bring to a simmer.
Cook for 10–12 minutes to let flavors absorb.
Garnish with coriander leaves.
Serve hot.

name: Palak Puri
steps: Blanch spinach in hot water for 2–3 minutes, then blend into a smooth puree.
In a bowl, mix flour, cumin seeds, turmeric, chili powder, and salt.
Add spinach puree and mix.
Knead into a stiff dough (add little water if needed).
Add 1 teaspoon oil and knead again.
Cover and rest for 10 minutes.
Divide into small balls.
Roll into small circles.
Heat oil in a deep pan on medium heat.
Fry puris until they puff and turn golden.
Remove and drain excess oil.
Serve hot.

name: Masala Dosa
steps: Heat oil in a pan and add mustard seeds.
Add sliced onions and green chili, sauté until soft.
Add turmeric and salt.
Add mashed potatoes and mix well to make the filling.
Cook for 3–4 minutes and set aside.
Heat a tawa on medium-high heat.
Pour a ladle of dosa batter and spread thinly in a circle.
Drizzle a little oil around the edges.
Cook until the base is crisp and golden.
Place potato filling in the center.
Fold the dosa over the filling.
Serve hot with chutney and sambar.

name: Rasmalai
steps: Boil 1 liter milk and add lemon juice to curdle it.
Strain to get chenna and wash it to remove sourness.
Squeeze excess water and knead into a smooth dough.
Make small flat discs from the dough.
Boil sugar and water to make syrup.
Add chenna discs and cook for 12–15 minutes until they expand.
In another pan, boil milk and reduce slightly.
Add sugar, cardamom, and saffron to make rabri.
Squeeze excess syrup from chenna discs and add them to rabri.
Garnish with nuts.
Chill and serve.

name: Homemade Buttermilk
steps: Add curd and water to a bowl or blender.
Whisk or blend until smooth and frothy.
Add salt, roasted cumin powder, and black salt.
Mix well.
Garnish with coriander leaves if desired.
Serve chilled.

name: Dal Dhokli
steps: Cook toor dal with turmeric until soft and mash well.
In a pan, heat oil or ghee and add mustard and cumin seeds.
Add curry leaves and chopped onion (optional), sauté briefly.
Add tomatoes and cook until soft.
Add chili powder, coriander powder, and salt.
Add tamarind pulp and cooked dal, mix well.
Add water and bring to a boil.
In a bowl, knead wheat flour with water into a soft dough.
Roll the dough thin and cut into small diamond or square pieces (dhokli).
Add dhokli pieces to boiling dal.
Cook for 10–15 minutes until dhokli is soft.
Garnish with coriander leaves.
Serve hot.

name: Claypot Vegetables with Cheese
ingredients: 1 cup mixed vegetables (carrot, beans, broccoli, cauliflower)
steps: Preheat oven to 180°C.
Blanch mixed vegetables in hot water for 3–4 minutes and drain.
Heat butter in a pan and sauté garlic and onion.
Add flour and cook for 1 minute.
Slowly add milk while stirring to make a smooth white sauce.
Add salt, pepper, and herbs.
Add vegetables and mix well.
Transfer mixture to a claypot or baking dish.
Top with grated cheese.
Bake for 15–20 minutes until cheese melts and turns slightly golden.
Serve hot.

name: Chocolate Balls
ingredients: 1/4 cup grated coconut or chocolate chips (for coating)
steps: Crush biscuits into fine crumbs.
Add cocoa powder and coffee powder, mix well.
Add condensed milk, melted butter, and vanilla extract.
Mix to form a soft dough-like mixture.
Add honey if extra richness is desired.
Take small portions and roll into balls.
Roll in grated coconut or chocolate chips.
Refrigerate for 10–15 minutes to set.
Serve chilled.

name: Potato Roast
steps: Boil potatoes, peel, and cut into cubes.
Heat oil in a pan on medium heat.
Add mustard seeds and let them crackle.
Add cumin seeds and curry leaves.
Add turmeric, chili powder, and coriander powder.
Add potato cubes and mix well.
Cook on medium heat, stirring occasionally.
Roast until potatoes turn golden and crispy.
Adjust salt and serve hot.

name: Kalan
steps: Cook raw banana or yam with turmeric and salt until soft.
Grind coconut, cumin seeds, and black pepper into a smooth paste.
Add the coconut paste to the cooked vegetables and mix well.
Cook on low heat for 5–7 minutes.
Add whisked curd and mix gently.
Cook on low heat without boiling to prevent curd from splitting.
Heat coconut oil in a small pan.
Add mustard seeds and let them crackle.
Add dried red chilies and curry leaves.
Pour the tempering over the curry.
Mix gently and serve.

name: Cheese Toast
steps: Heat a pan or preheat oven/toaster.
Spread butter on bread slices.
Place grated cheese evenly on top.
Sprinkle salt, pepper, and optional spices.
Toast on pan (covered) or bake until cheese melts.
Cook until bread is crisp and cheese is bubbly.
Serve hot.

name: Mango Jam
steps: Peel and blend mangoes to make smooth pulp.
Add mango pulp to a pan.
Add sugar and lemon juice.
Cook on medium heat, stirring continuously.
Simmer for 15–20 minutes until thickened.
Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.
Check consistency (it thickens more on cooling).
Let it cool completely.
Store in a clean, airtight jar.
Serve with bread or desserts.

name: Peda
steps: Heat ghee in a pan on low heat.
Add milk powder and sauté lightly.
Add condensed milk and mix well.
Cook on low heat, stirring continuously until thick and dough-like.
Add cardamom powder and mix.
Let the mixture cool slightly.
Take small portions and shape into pedas.
Garnish with chopped nuts if desired.
Serve or store.

name: Vegetable Fried Rice
ingredients: 1/2 cup mixed vegetables (carrot, beans, capsicum, cabbage)
steps: Heat oil in a wok or pan on high heat.
Add chopped garlic and sauté briefly.
Add vegetables and stir-fry for 2–3 minutes.
Add cooked rice and mix well.
Add soy sauce, salt, and black pepper.
Stir-fry on high heat for 3–4 minutes.
Add spring onions and mix.
Serve hot.

name: Green Chutney
steps: Wash coriander and mint leaves thoroughly.
Add all ingredients to a blender.
Add a little water to help blend.
Blend into a smooth paste.
Adjust salt and lemon juice.
Serve fresh.

name: Falooda
steps: Boil milk and add sugar, then let it cool.
Cook falooda sev according to instructions and drain.
Soak basil seeds in water until they swell.
In a tall glass, add soaked basil seeds.
Add cooked falooda sev.
Pour sweetened milk.
Add rose syrup.
Top with a scoop of ice cream.
Garnish with nuts.
Serve chilled.

name: Adai
steps: Soak rice and all dals together for 3–4 hours.
Grind with red chilies and cumin seeds to a coarse batter.
Add salt, chopped onion, and curry leaves.
Mix well (no fermentation needed).
Heat a tawa on medium heat.
Pour a ladle of batter and spread thick like a pancake.
Drizzle oil around edges.
Cook until golden and crisp, then flip.
Cook the other side until done.
Serve hot with chutney or jaggery.

name: Mango Pickle
steps: Wash and completely dry the mango pieces.
Mix mango pieces with salt and turmeric.
Let it rest for a few hours or overnight.
Add chili powder and ground mustard seeds.
Mix well.
Heat mustard oil until hot, then cool slightly.
Pour oil over the mango mixture.
Mix well and transfer to a clean, dry jar.
Store in sunlight for a few days for best flavor.
Stir occasionally.
Use once flavors are developed.

name: Apple Cardamom Halwa
steps: Heat ghee in a pan on medium heat.
Add grated apples and cook for 5–7 minutes until soft.
Add milk and cook until it reduces.
Add sugar and mix well.
Cook until mixture thickens and leaves the sides of the pan.
Add cardamom powder and mix.
Add chopped nuts if desired.
Serve warm.

name: Sandwich Dhokla
steps: In a bowl, mix gram flour, water, lemon juice, sugar, turmeric, salt, and oil to form a smooth batter.
Let it rest for 10 minutes.
Add baking soda or Eno just before steaming and mix gently.
Pour half the batter into a greased steaming tray.
Steam for 5–6 minutes until partially set.
Spread green chutney evenly on top.
Pour remaining batter over the chutney layer.
Steam again for 10–12 minutes until fully cooked.
Let it cool slightly and cut into pieces.
Prepare tempering with oil, mustard seeds, sesame seeds, chilies, and curry leaves.
Pour tempering over dhokla.
Garnish with coriander leaves and serve.

name: Pomegranate Juice
steps: Add pomegranate seeds and water to a blender.
Blend until juice is extracted.
Strain to remove seeds.
Add lemon juice and sweetener if desired.
Mix well.
Serve chilled.

name: Baked Paneer in Spinach with Tomato Gravy
steps: Preheat oven to 180°C.
Heat oil in a pan and sauté onions until soft.
Add garlic and ginger-garlic paste, cook for 1 minute.
Add tomato puree and cook until oil separates.
Add turmeric, chili powder, coriander powder, and salt.
Add spinach puree and mix well.
Simmer for 5–7 minutes.
Add paneer cubes and mix gently.
Transfer to a baking dish.
Add cream and mix lightly (optional).
Top with grated cheese if desired.
Bake for 15–20 minutes until slightly golden.
Serve hot.

name: Malai Kofta
steps: Mix paneer, mashed potatoes, cornflour, salt, and nuts.
Shape into small balls (kofta).
Heat oil and deep-fry koftas until golden. Set aside.
Heat oil or butter in a pan and sauté onions until golden.
Add ginger-garlic paste and cook for 1 minute.
Add tomato puree and cook until oil separates.
Add turmeric, chili powder, coriander powder, and salt.
Add water and simmer for 5–7 minutes.
Add cream and garam masala.
Add koftas just before serving.
Garnish with coriander leaves.
Serve hot.

name: Dum Aloo
steps: Prick boiled potatoes with a fork.
Heat oil and lightly fry potatoes until golden. Set aside.
In the same pan, add cumin seeds and let them crackle.
Add chopped onions and sauté until golden.
Add ginger-garlic paste and cook for 1 minute.
Add tomato puree and cook until oil separates.
Add turmeric, chili powder, coriander powder, and salt.
Add water and mix well.
Add fried potatoes and coat with gravy.
Cover and cook on low heat (dum) for 10–15 minutes.
Add garam masala and mix.
Garnish with coriander leaves.
Serve hot.

name: Dahi Vada
steps: Grind soaked urad dal into a thick batter.
Whisk batter to make it light and fluffy.
Heat oil and fry small vadas until golden.
Remove and soak vadas in warm water for 5 minutes.
Squeeze gently to remove excess water.
Whisk curd with salt and sugar until smooth.
Place vadas in a bowl and pour curd over them.
Add tamarind chutney on top.
Sprinkle cumin powder and chili powder.
Garnish with coriander leaves.
Serve chilled or at room temperature.

name: Basundi
steps: Heat milk in a heavy-bottom pan on medium heat.
Bring to a boil and then reduce heat.
Simmer while stirring frequently to prevent burning.
Continue until milk reduces to about half and becomes thick.
Add sugar and mix well.
Add cardamom powder and saffron.
Cook for another 5–10 minutes until slightly thickened.
Turn off heat and let it cool.
Garnish with chopped nuts.
Serve warm or chilled.
